motion_desc: Original main motion — introduces new business before the assembly.
motion_special_types: Special types of original main motions
amendment_desc: Subsidiary motion — proposes a change to the pending question.
amendment_placeholder: Edit the text above to reflect your proposed changes...
point_of_order_desc: A Point of Order calls attention to a breach of rules or procedural error. The chair will rule on your concern.
minutes_correction_desc: Describe the correction needed to the minutes from the previous meeting.
minutes_correction_placeholder: On page X, line Y should read...
incidental_main_desc: Choose an incidental main motion template. You can edit the text before introducing it.
incidental_motions_desc: Choose an incidental motion to bring before the chair.
subsidiary_desc: Subsidiary motion — applies to the pending question.
subsidiary_postpone_indef_text: I move to postpone the question indefinitely
subsidiary_commit_motion: to refer the pending question to a committee
subsidiary_postpone_def_motion: to postpone the pending question to a definite time
subsidiary_limit_debate_motion: to limit or extend the limits of debate
subsidiary_previous_question_motion: to close debate and immediately vote on the pending question
subsidiary_lay_on_table_motion: to lay the pending question on the table
privileged_heading_orders: Call for Orders of the Day
privileged_desc: Privileged motion — takes precedence over all pending business.
privileged_privilege_placeholder: I rise to a question of privilege affecting...
privileged_adjourn_note: This motion, if adopted, will immediately adjourn the meeting.
privileged_orders_note: This demand requires the assembly to conform to the agenda.
bring_back_desc: Bring-back motion — returns a previously decided or tabled question.
bring_back_no_items: No items available for this action.
appeal_heading: Appeal the Decision of the Chair
appeal_desc: An appeal reverses the chair's ruling by a vote of the assembly. Each member may speak once; the chair may speak twice. A tie vote sustains the chair.
parl_inquiry_desc: A Parliamentary Inquiry asks the chair a question about procedure or the effect of a motion. The chair will respond to your inquiry.
parl_inquiry_placeholder: I rise to a parliamentary inquiry: Is it in order to...
request_info_desc: A Request for Information (Point of Information) asks a factual question about the business at hand. The chair or a designated member will respond.
request_info_placeholder: I rise for a point of information: ...
suspend_rules_desc: Suspend the Rules allows the assembly to do something that would otherwise be out of order under the standing rules.
suspend_rules_requirements: Requires a second. Not debatable. Two-thirds vote required.
suspend_rules_purpose_nonmember: Allow a non-member to address the assembly
suspend_rules_purpose_order: Take up business out of scheduled order
suspend_rules_purpose_committee: Consider a matter without referral to committee
suspend_rules_placeholder: I move to suspend the rules in order to...
withdraw_desc_mover: You are the mover of this motion. If there is no objection, the motion will be withdrawn by unanimous consent. If objected to, a majority vote is required.
withdraw_desc_other: Only the mover may request to withdraw. This will ask the chair to request unanimous consent to withdraw the motion.
pre_withdraw_desc: The chair has not recognized your motion yet. You may unilaterally withdraw or reformulate it.
